item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Noting that the image collage contains portions of Otto Dix's War Cripples artwork, which is copyrighted by the Artists Rights Society. As such, it has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
No. The "Good Doctor" was probably a Doctorate of art or something. Probably taught Art Theory or Art History (Art History seems like a good candidate, actually…) at some German University. He was a literal wizard on the side. His last act was telling young German hipsters "We aren't cool we will never be cool." That's my take on it, anyway.

Unrelated: the blackboxed SCP is totally SCP-2140-D in my headcanon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "The good doctor"? Is this a Wondertainment SCP as well as AWCY??

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Fun piece that tells a good story.
Glad I could be your +100!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm just wondering, according to the previous revision to the page, the only documented change was an update to image formatting, however, this edit also removed part of the last addenda and removed the context of the last footnote, was this intentional?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Love the pun at the end.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It is. They're listed in alphabetical order, because the actual holes are not ordered in the manner we're familiar with.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
For that last collapsable bit:

Are Cool We Yet? I thought it was are we cool yet.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If you're on a computer, you should be able to hover your mouse over the footnote to circumvent that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ugh, another SCP with footnote spam. I'm not interested in reading an article that makes me scroll down every 5 seconds

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's the answer to the question AWCY? poses.

+ show block
– hide block

The answer is nein.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What is the pun exactly?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've loved this for months, but I only just got the pun. Wow.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: AWCY? done right is one of my favorite GsoI, but I agree that this tale would have been so much better with two wonderful, funderful contract attorneys from Dr. Wondertainment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Addendum 2 really makes this piece. I heart artist squabbles! Also, this brings up an intergenerational element: how long has AWCY? been around exactly?

item.title: ^^^ THIS ^^^
item.content:encoded: So much of the AWCY? hate seems to be a dislike of execution rather than anything inherent in the concept of the GoI. It's sort of like hating any humanoid/cat/sphere/fill-in-the-blank SCP simply because it's so easy to write one badly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This. I like the idea of a GOI whose purpose/message is not so easily understandable.

If you want to complain that there are too many new SCPs where AWCY feels tacked on, or that have the same "Art that kills you" schtick, that's a different issue.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
And this is why I like AWCY as a GoI. The Global Occult Coalition seek the destruction of anomalous materials to protect the world; the Chaos Insurgency instead want to USE things like SCPs to take over the world (maybe?), the Church of the Broken God want to fix their deity, etc.

What do they all have in common? Internal unity of purpose. That's the one thing AWCY lacks. They're a generalized "art movement" that wants to somehow leave their mark on the world, but how they do that and what that mark even MEANS is so incredibly, wildly different from cell to cell, person to person. And so are their skills.

This works so well with my (and apparently, some others') headcannon that certain parts of AWCY actually are able to construct their own anomalous things, but it's just as likely - if not moreso - that they find/commission them, and no matter what it's originally supposed to be for, they use it for their own self-professed crusades.

Also, this particular thing is just weird and well written.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't get it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, thanks, I'm glad you like the SCP itself on its own merits. Though, I'm somewhat perplexed at the people who feel that AWCY is "shoehorned" into this, since this idea grew outward from the idea of the old mentor and some younguns who didn't get it. The nature of the SCP grew out of that dynamic, looking for something that could have shallow and deep interpretations/motives.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The characterization of the AWCY artists as being naive amateurs who don't really have any technical ability - or comprehension of the forces that they're dealing with - is pretty dead on with my perception of them. But it's also part of the reason that I don't really want to write about AWCY, or read about AWCY, or acknowledge that AWCY exists.

But in the end I like the execution and the SCP, so I'm gonna upvote but AWCY delenda est.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Aesthetically this is closer to Dada then any of the other AWCY SCP's, I mean it's giant collage and it's even got a little shrine to Dadaism and German Expressionism and Cubism and all the other stuff that the Nazi's tried to stamp out built into it. And I'm ambivalent towards this because while I like the visual it paints, the monument to censorship/redaction is distinct enough to stand on it's own, and putting in the other stuff robs it off it's uniqueness and puts it perilously close to the usual AWCY pitfalls (Take existing modern artwork - Pointlessly alter the idea to it's most harmful and anomalous extreme - write "AWCY" somewhere on it.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Great writing, makes sense in the world, AWCY? feels a bit shoehorned in, but not too badly. It actually makes a lot more sense as something to do that most of the AWCY? posts.

And now you've introduced Thule and people who know about Thule. Hmmm…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think it's that last bit that makes me like this so much. My own AWCY? attempt (SCP-1999) took the form of "they know this effect exists, either don't know or don't care how it works, and are using it to send a message to the Foundation". They've got the ability as a movement to use abnormal stuff in their installations, so it's great that they get it wrong sometimes because they're more dedicated to their art than to understanding anything they're doing.

Whereas you, good sir, appear quite dedicated to them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
at the first AWCY? reference I felt "oh god not another".

But the conversation that followed added a nice bit of history I can believe, and was intrigued by.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I guess I should have said it, but I was more curious about the *more* part of the "more heavy handed bullshit". I just completely failed to communicate that.

As I said, I was just curious about why you disliked the group so much. If you'd rather not say, or go into it I won't pester you further about it. I just enjoyed the discussion spawned from Digiwizzard's post, and was hoping for similar insight from another perspective.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well my take is that AWCY? is a school of (art) philosophy that inherits from Dada, much as Surrealism did. So they are their own thing, just as Surrealism is distinct from Dada. This (and my last one SCP-1800) was inspired by thinking about how AWCY? fits into art history in general. 1800 was more a glimpse of how they might have found inspiration, this one is hinting around how they might have developed into a movement. Dr. ██████ is implied to have been involved in the art scene in the Wiemar era, which means he'd be touching on Dadaism, Surrealism, Expressionism and all the other modern art forms the Nazis loathed, combine that with esoteric knowledge, you have a perfect mentor figure to nurture the formation of AWCY? But then the new kids show up, don't get it, and all hell breaks loose :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Heh, I don't mind… to be honest, you hating it and Bright upvoting made my day back then - I wrote it as a Fuck You Challenge Accepted of sorts to Digiwizzard, and well, you are on the same platform with him there :3

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I know dude, and I don't like it. Not even as a lampoon.

But I still love you guys. My unstoppable hate only goes so far as fictional art groups.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sorts, heavy handed was sort of the point here. Remember - that was the part of AWCY? that took action in Occupy protests. How many not heavy handed would be revolutionaries have you seen?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I can't say I'm tired of AWCY, only it seems that they're really hot right now and everyone's doing them. So I was just a tad disappointed that this ended up being AWCY; I was hoping for an art-related SCP that wasn't related to them. Which didn't stop me from upvoting because I don't hate them.

That said, this doesn't quite strike me as dada. But then, I'm not certain I understand dada. (I'm not certain that anyone does, but that's beside the point.) It doesn't quite feel like them, though as a glimpse of their methods, it's astounding.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The "heavy handed bullshit" comment was on an entry where nightsticks and other symbols of authority were turned into penises. I think it's pretty self explanatory.

My opinion doesn't change the score on this more than one point like anyone else's. Anyone else is free to like or dislike the ACWY entries as they please.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm having a hard time thinking of a SCP involving a GOI that *couldn't* have the GOI removed or substituted and leave it mostly unchanged.

I've seen you post on another AWCY? entry that it was "heavy handed bullshit", and otherwise post about how you think the group sucks.

I'd love to hear more about your specific issues with the group. Would you mind posting something in the AWCY? thread in the general forums about it or pm'ing me? (Not looking to change your mind, I'm just interested in the view point.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think I could have worded what I have in mind better. I think I'll do an edit when I'm not on my iPod.

EDIT: Done, reworded that bit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: AWCY was my favorite GOI before this, it's even better after this. Very good characterization for them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the attempt backfiring on the AWCY members, and the AWCY members throwing around "fascism" without really knowing what it is (either that, or them thinking they can bamboozle an anti-fascist simply by spouting the word "fascism").

While approximately half of new items are known to have been destroyed or discarded within a 10km radius of SCP-1803-1 within 12 months prior to incorporation,

How could the Foundation possibly know that, unless there's nothing but Foundation controlled property within a 10km radius around Sector-23? If it said "items which could plausibly have been destroyed with a 10km radius with the 12 month prior to incorporation", that I could believe.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Both good suggestions, adopted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I would have upvoted if it was a different group from AWCY. The climatic pun is just fluff.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I kind of meant it as a joke about people's interpretation of the group's "catch-phrase" as a plea for approval, but what I said barely comes off as a joke now that I look at it. >_>

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I personally think this is the best AWCY related SCP to date.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not according to the artist…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
with ceilings at least 15m in height.

Just say 15m. Or better yet roll that dimension in with the width and height.

To prevent the spread of SCP-1803-1 beyond SCP-1803, cables suspending SCP-1803 should be no greater than 15mm in diameter and should be spaced no closer than 0.5m together at their anchor points.

Remove "To prevent the spread of SCP-1803-1 beyond SCP-1803". The containment procedures don't need to explain the "why" for anything they specify. The reader should be able to deduce the "why" while reading the description. You want the reader to think "Why the hell is that necessary?" and then later think "Oooohhhh…now I see." For instance, you get it right here: "Absolutely no SCP decommissioning is to take place within a 10km radius of SCP-1803."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I guess they're cool now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was passively interested until the interview. That secured my upvote.

I have rather enjoyed most of the AWCY? submissions. It's interesting to see a similar underlying principal interpreted in so many different ways.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Heh ,yes.
They became art.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
YES

Also, utterly disagree there, Sorts. The transcript of the conversation is what made me upvote so hard.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I won't tell you you're wrong, since we are all entitled to our own opinions and can downvote for whatever reason we damn well please, but I do register a very strong disagreement as to this characterization:

You could remove all references to AWCY? from this piece and not change in it the least.

IMHO without the narrative context this would be a much lesser piece. Might as well get rid of the historical context as well and just have a collage that eats people. That would be just as good…. Sorry, I just don't think so.

Unless what you're saying is that if the AWCY? was replaced by some random group unrelated to anything else… In which case I still don't think so. Besides being a pointless change, it would eliminate the climatic pun.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think this is exactly how do you a supernatural art philosophical terrorist group. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Nope. I wavered on the possibilities for a brief moment but I'm just flat out done with them.

You could remove all references to AWCY? from this piece and not change in it the least. We really don't need to create SCPs to invoke or improve a lousy GOI, nor use a lousy GOI as some sort of meta narrative for every art themed SCP ever.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Beyond not being in the chosen place, it seems to have worked pretty well for them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like AWCY, but I always thought an article displaying one of their projects backfiring would be interesting. Good job.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Any specific suggestions as to tone? (And, yeah, I know it's a bit long… and this is after a bunch of cutting.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Congratulations. You mentioned Are We Cool Yet? and I immediately wanted to hate it. But then I read it and ended up liking it. Take this upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There are some minor tone issues, and I think it could be trimmed down a bit, but it's compelling. I'm not sure what I just read but it got me thinking. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this one, not a big flashy AWCY scp, but subtle, and it's good. you need to change those 'moths' to months.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Theres a house with collages like this in my town…..uh oh.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: An AWCY? SCP for people who can't stand AWCY?
